title: A plot hole in EoE that I dont think anyone has noticed before
display_title: A plot hole in EoE that I dont think anyone has noticed before
wikitext:
[]: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 11:23 am Recently I realized that there was a plot hole in EoE that no one has seemingly noticed before. It's mainly concerning the climax of the first half, where Misato dies while Asuka battles the Eva series. I realized that the timeline of that scene doesn't really add up. I mean, think about it: Misato tells Asuka that she needs to fight the Eva series, and immediately afterwards Asuka remarks that she only has 3 minutes 30 seconds of internal power left. Then, Misato gets shot, and she gives Shinji a big motivational speech before dying. Then, after all that, it once again cuts back to Asuka, who's near the beginning of her battle, and a few seconds later it shows Shinji in the Eva cage. The problem is, the scene with Misato lasted more than 4 minutes long! By the time Shinji got to that cage, Asuka should have run out of power already. I did the math on the Eva battle, and there were only 30 seconds of offscreen action that could have possibly happened there. Nowhere near enough time for Misato to give her whole dying speech. Am I right, or is there something I'm missing? This is really bugging me, and I hope I'm not just going crazy.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by IgRAzm » Wed Dec 02, 2020 11:55 am Well, it's quite simple, BusterMachine4. Just like the Fundamental Anime Logic Law states to us, it is prohibited for the time to pass while motivational speeches are taking place. When you consider that fact, the timeline makes sense.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 12:02 pm IgRAzm wrote: Well, it's quite simple, BusterMachine4. Just like the Fundamental Anime Logic Law states to us, it is prohibited for the time to pass while motivational speeches are taking place. When you consider that fact, the timeline makes sense.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by IgRAzm » Wed Dec 02, 2020 12:09 pm BusterMachine4 wrote: Don't forget transformation sequences, too. I guess you're right, but I thought Eva was trying to be better than that. Usually, they try to make actions have real consequences, so it's weird that they'd make such a big oversight for the purpose of dramatic effect.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 12:26 pm Okay, so I was looking through the wiki's resource on early drafts of EoE, hoping to find an explanation for the issue, and I think I may have actually found one! It talks about an early draft of the Eva battle, that was almost completely different from the final version. There were many changes to the scene, but the one I'm most concerned with is concerning Unit-02's power cable. Originally Asuka's cable was actually supposed to be severed by one of the evil Evas, after Misato's death! I guess in the final draft, they decided it would be better if SEELE's goons severed the power cable, not realizing that it created a plot hole. It still bugs me, but at least I can understand it now.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by IgRAzm » Wed Dec 02, 2020 12:33 pm BusterMachine4 wrote: I guess in the final draft, they decided it would be better if SEELE's goons severed the power cable, not realizing that it created a plot hole. It still bugs me, but at least I can understand it now.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 7:05 pm I'm pretty sure it's just non-chronological editing in service of the story. I honestly don't think it's weird at all. Or thinngs we see are expanded or shortened from how they "actually" happen. EoE definitely isn't a film aiming for realism.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 7:46 pm kuribo-04 wrote: I'm pretty sure it's just non-chronological editing in service of the story. I honestly don't think it's weird at all. Or thinngs we see are expanded or shortened from how they "actually" happen.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Dec 02, 2020 10:40 pm It is an interesting catch. I had never noticed despite maybe having seen the film like 50 times lol

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Thu Dec 03, 2020 10:00 am All right, having been apparently the first person to notice this issue, I feel like it’s my responsibility to fanwank an in-universe explanation. So, the core of the problem is the scene where Misato contacts Asuka on the phone. The dialogue seems to indicate that Misato is calling Asuka while she’s running on internal power, which doesn’t make sense as I already established. But what if she actually wasn’t? My theory now is that the scene is actually being edited somewhat weirdly, and that Misato called Asuka while she was still rampaging through the JSDF forces with her power cable intact. After that, there was some time we didn’t see before Asuka’s cable was severed, enough time for Misato’s dying speech. Asuka responding to Misato’s call would then be recontextualized as remembering what she said earlier. So that’s my explanation. Hopefully I can stop being so bothered by it now.

[]: Re: A plot hole in EoE that I don't think anyone has noticed before             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 03, 2020 11:34 pm That's a really good catch, I can't believe it got overlooked after all this time! Your explanation for it sounds spot-on, it sounds logical to me that the power cable was meant to be severed by the harpies and not by Seele's forces, but they forgot about this! It would also explain one of the things in EoE that bugged me. We already know that there are multiple power attachments for an Eva, so why couldn't Asuka grab hold of a second power cable and get rid of the obvious power problem when she's just dealing with the JSSDF forces (mere pests to her) and the MP Evas are taking their time to descend? It's because the power cable was meant to be taken down during the harpy fight, giving Asuka no opening to find a new power cable. And there are fights where an Eva is on a 1-minute rather than 5-minute power limit, which we see for Eva-02 in Episode 08 and Eva-01 in Episode 19, which would put Asuka at an even worse disadvantage.
